tract map for a parcet in your jurisdiction when the rnap was

iinanciat interest in the decision. (section 84308(c).)

2/ section 84308 applies only to officers of appointed boards or
commissions with reipect to proceedings involving licenses,
permits, or other entitlenents for use. (Requlation 18438'1;

According to your facts, the planning conroj.ssion proceeding

conmission proeeedingT because of a $3oO contribution received from

The city councit is now considering the issue. section

before the city council.'/

is not a party but who actively supports or opposes a particular
decision in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use and who has a financial interest in the
decision.
4/ 
This conclusion is consistent with the rule applied to
conflict-of-interest situations in Regulation lAzo-0.1 (copy
enclosed) which precludes participation only before the official's
agency or an agency under the budgetary control of the official's
agency. (Brown Advice Letter, No. A-84-131r' Ilarron Advice Letter,

sections.question: Does Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act preclude you from speaking against a tentative tract map for a parcel in your jurisdiction when the map is considered by the city council?
sections.conclusion: Section 84308 does not prevent you from acting in your private capacity before the city council. The disqualification requirement under Section 84308 applies only to proceedings before the officer's own agency (the planning commission), not to subsequent proceedings before a different agency (the city council).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a planning commissioner who received a $300 campaign contribution from a participant in a planning commission proceeding can speak against that matter when it is subsequently considered by the city council.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that Section 84308's disqualification requirement does not apply to the city council proceeding because the conflict-of-interest rules only preclude participation before the official's own agency, not different agencies.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Advice letter confirming prior telephone advice; question and conclusion clearly identifiable from letter content; minor OCR artifacts present but do not affect meaning extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act preclude you from speaking against a tentative tract map for a parcel in your jurisdiction when the map is considered by the city council?

CONCLUSION: Section 84308 does not prevent you from acting in your private capacity before the city council. The disqualification requirement under Section 84308 applies only to proceedings before the officer's own agency (the planning commission), not to subsequent proceedings before a different agency (the city council).

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding whether a planning commissioner must disqualify from speaking on a matter before the city council due to a $300 campaign contribution received from a participant in the prior planning commission proceeding on the same matter.
